chunk_summary: This section outlines a shared care plan template for nutrition counselling, emphasizing standardized referral note language, interprofessional collaboration, and follow-up monitoring, with specific guidance for India’s ICDS, PM POSHAN, and RBSK programs.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a standardized shared care plan for nutrition counselling.
Learn how to document and communicate key nutrition diagnoses and barriers in referral notes.
Incorporate India-specific programs like ICDS, PM POSHAN, and RBSK into care plans.
Apply teach-back methods to confirm family understanding of shared care plans.
key_facts: A shared care plan should include primary nutrition diagnosis, severity indicators, dominant barrier domains, and specific tasks for the next clinician.
Examples of referral tasks include assessing oral-motor safety, providing culturally appropriate meal plans, and addressing ARFID-related anxiety.
India-specific actions include ICDS enrolment for under-6s, school meal access under PM POSHAN, and RBSK screening for deficiencies or developmental concerns.
Follow-up responsibilities should be clearly assigned, e.g., pediatrics monitoring growth every 2–4 weeks until stabilization.
Teach-back methods should be used to confirm family understanding of the care plan.
reference_quote: In India, add linkage actions: Anganwadi/ICDS enrolment status for under-6s, school meal access under PM POSHAN, and whether RBSK screening/referral is indicated for deficiencies or developmental concerns.
document_key_topics: India-specific nutrition programs (ICDS, PM POSHAN, RBSK)
